personality_one_liner: A structured, highly legible, and earthy tool that replaces complexity with clear, sequential guidance for farmers in the field.

spacing.rule: Avoid dense layouts. Ensure massive touch targets (minimum 48pt) and generous vertical padding. Use a strict single-column flow to prevent cognitive overload. Eliminate horizontal scrolling.

dont's: Do not use glass or blur. Glare in outdoor agricultural environments makes frosted glass completely unreadable. Stick to solid, high-contrast surfaces.

screens.name: Auth / Onboarding
screens.purpose: Secure JWT login via Phone or Email with an upfront language toggle (English/Kannada).

states.loading: Centered spinner over soft earthy background

screens.layout: Top area: Ragi field hero image with gradient scrim. Middle: Massive language toggle pill. Bottom: Large input field (min-height 56pt) and a sticky, full-width 'Send OTP' button pinned to the bottom above the keyboard. No hidden menus.

screens.name: Home Dashboard
screens.purpose: A glanceable soil health summary for multiple plots and a primary entry point for scanning new reports.

states.empty: Illustration of hands holding soil + 'Add Your First Plot' button

screens.layout: Vertical single-column layout. Top: Farm location and weather summary. Middle: Plot Health Cards (large, color-coded status, high-contrast text). Bottom: A sticky, massive FAB (Floating Action Button) or persistent bottom action row labeled 'Scan Soil Card' using a camera icon. Avoid dense grids; stack everything vertically.

screens.name: Soil Card Scanner (AI OCR)
screens.purpose: Guided, step-by-step camera flow to capture the physical soil card and answer AI survey questions.
screens.layout: Full-screen camera with a dark semi-transparent overlay defining the scan area. Post-capture, present survey questions ONE AT A TIME in a vertical card format. Use oversized 'Yes'/'No' or graphical choice buttons. Provide visual audio-playback cues ('Press to read').

screens.name: Fertilizer Prescription
screens.purpose: Translates complex lab data into simple, step-by-step organic fertilizer applications.

states.error: Failed to generate plan - Retry button

screens.layout: Replace data tables with sequential, task-based cards (e.g., 'Step 1: Add Compost'). Use literal icons (e.g., a bag, a leaf). Include a historical soil trend chart (N/P/K) wrapped in a horizontal scroll but keep it highly simplified (large data points, heavy line weights) for outdoor visibility.

screens.name: Community & Labs Map
screens.purpose: Locate nearest soil testing labs, fertilizer shops, and view anonymized community plot health.
screens.layout: Map view taking up the top 60% of the screen. Bottom 40% is a Bottom Sheet (`@gorhom/bottom-sheet`) containing a vertical list of nearest labs/shops with high-contrast 'Navigate' and 'Call' buttons. Ensure touch targets on the map pins are inflated to prevent fat-finger errors.

auth_hero.alt: Lush ragi millet field under clear sky

map_placeholder.alt: Aerial view of a rural village and fields

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the color palette. Do NOT use blue, indigo, or purple. Stick to the earthy greens, ochre, terracotta, and off-whites.
Implement the 'iOS-Native Clean' personality but optimize it for low literacy: replace all thin fonts with medium/bold weights (min 500w) and enforce massive touch targets (minimum 48pt height).
Avoid glassmorphism/blur completely. This app will be used outdoors in direct sunlight; frosted glass effects wash out and reduce legibility.
Ensure all text rendered over images uses the gradient scrim pattern (top-to-bottom transparent to >=70% surfaceInverse) to guarantee contrast.
Use literal, easily recognizable iconography (MaterialCommunityIcons: leaf, water, map-marker, camera) instead of abstract symbols. Avoid hidden hamburger menus.
Build the dashboard as a sequential vertical flow. Eliminate complex multi-column grids or dense data tables.
Include a prominent Language Toggle (English/Kannada) early in the Auth flow and in the user profile.
Mock JWT Auth, AI OCR extraction, map rendering, and AI fertilizer prescription generations.
When rendering historical soil charts, use heavy line weights and large data points for readability in high glare.
